title: Model Lab Research Signal Clarity, Color Bands, Driver Explanations, and Snapshot/Report Integration

overlay_label_cleanup.subtitle: Relative model research signal based on historical new construction patterns.
overlay_label_cleanup.safe_explanation: This shows relative research signal bands. It does not show exact development probability.

color_band_changes.higher_research_signal: warm orange/gold marker with strongest emphasis

how_signal_is_calculated_explained.plain_english: Markers are relative research bands based on internal model ranking and contextual drivers, not probabilities.
map_reading_guidance: Brighter markers show higher relative research signal.
Signal bands are based on internal model ranking, not exact probability.
Drivers explain which parcel context features contributed.
Missing official data means this remains research-only.
Use this to guide questions, not decisions.

right_panel_selected_marker_behavior.no_marker_selected: Model Lab Intelligence explains current best model, overlay meaning, colors, helpful feature groups, missing data, and caveats.
right_panel_selected_marker_behavior.marker_selected: Selected Model Research Context shows signal band, relative rank band, parcel id, top contextual drivers, model version, data quality, why highlighted, what it does not mean, and caveat.

executive_report_integration.shown_when: Planning Snapshot was saved from Model Lab mode
executive_report_integration.required_caveat_present: This is an internal research preview. It does not show exact parcel development probability and is not an official parcel score.

documentation_updates: README Model Lab overview updated for Phase 23C
nontechnical model explanation updated with relative signal bands
what-not-to-claim sheet updated with approved Development Research Signal wording
expected Q&A explains Development Research Signal
model readiness and QA report updated with Phase 23C research-signal caveats

safety_verification.notes: Safety flag names such as exact_probability_available remain in typed API contracts and backend responses as false guardrail metadata; no exact probability values or raw scores are displayed.

smoke_test_results.browser_console: One existing opaque 'r' error was observed at localhost; no visible Model Lab, snapshot, or Methodology breakage.

remaining_known_limitations: Development Research Signal remains internal research only and should guide staff questions, not decisions.
The preview overlay uses safe centroid markers from existing internal research outputs; it does not expose raw scores or probabilities.

Official utility capacity, official school capacity/enrollment, official rezoning records, and countywide development pipeline data are still needed before any public-facing model signal is considered.
A persistent opaque one-character browser console error remains from the local map/runtime smoke test and should be tracked separately if it becomes user-visible.
